steps.title: Use vertex form and determine the sign of a
steps.content: With vertex $(9, -14)$ the parabola is $y = a(x - 9)^2 - 14$. Since it crosses the x-axis at two points and the vertex is below the x-axis, the parabola opens upward, so $a > 0$.

steps.title: Express $a + b + c$ using $f(1)$
steps.content:
In $y = ax^2 + bx + c$, plugging $x = 1$ gives $f(1) = a + b + c$. Using vertex form:
$$a + b + c = a(1 - 9)^2 - 14 = 64a - 14$$

steps.title: Apply the constraint $a > 0$
steps.content: Since $a > 0$, we need $64a - 14 > -14$, so $a + b + c > -14$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $-23$: less than $-14$ — impossible, eliminate.
  • $-19$: less than $-14$ — impossible, eliminate.
  • $-14$: would require $a = 0$, not a parabola — eliminate.
  • $-12$: gives $64a - 14 = -12$, so $a = \frac{1}{32} > 0$ — works.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
